disputed.description: Strong improvement. Deterministic corpus ordering is a real reproducibility concern, especially for cross-OS CI and audit-log artifact diffs where filesystem traversal order can vary. The recommendation is specific, low-cost, and actionable: define a canonical ordering, such as sorting by vector_id, and require corpus_loader to emit vectors in that order. This directly supports reliability and stable review artifacts without adding meaningful complexity.
ReDoS is a genuine and well-known risk when testing regex-based sanitizers with adversarial inputs. Timeouts are trivial to add and prevent CI hangs. Domain-appropriate insight.
Valid consistency concern - if differential runner sanitizes env, single-shot should too for parity. Specific and actionable, though impact may be modest if CI env is already controlled.
Concrete technical gap - jq will fail on # comments. If the schema-major comment convention is specified, the loader must handle it. Specific and actionable.
Real concern about flock contention with parallel jobs. Worth addressing but the proposed solution is vague. Could also be solved by per-worker logs merged at end.
Important clarification - multi-turn aggregation semantics are central to detecting cumulative attacks. Stateless subprocess model creates real ambiguity that needs resolution.
Reasonable suggestion but premature optimization at 50 vectors. Easy to add later when needed. Nice-to-have rather than critical gap.
Valid edge case - encoded payloads could bypass plaintext linting. However, decoding all possible encodings is an arms race; the proposed AST primitive approach is sound but adds complexity.
Decent UX improvement but low impact - dependency errors are usually self-evident. Standard hygiene rather than domain insight.
Legitimate operational gap but low urgency. Baseline rotation is mentioned without procedure; documenting SOP is valuable but not blocking.

phase1_partial.note: 2 of 6 Phase 1 calls failed: gpt-review and gpt-skeptic (gpt-5.5-pro). Opus + Gemini paths succeeded. Likely the #783 follow-up class (legacy adapter /v1/responses parsing for reasoning-model output shapes); under investigation.
